definition:
ἀγοραῖος, -ον 
 (ἀγορά), 
   1. frequenting the ἀγορά , a lounger in the ἀγορά  (Xen., al.): Act.17:5. 
   2. In late writers (Strab., al.), proper to the ἀγορά : ἀγοραῖοι (sc. ἥμεραι) ἀγονται (cf. Lat. conventus agere), court-days are kept, Act.19:38 (for exx. of both usages, see MM, VGT, see word).
 † (AS)
